reasoning: I'm voting Shantanu Thakur because he delivered what I voted for in 2019—CAA is law now, my family has citizenship papers, and the rules are finally being implemented after four years of waiting. Yes, only fourteen certificates so far, but that's the beginning; there are lakhs of us, this takes time. Shantanu was with Modi when CAA passed, he pushed for these rules, and he kept his word. TMC opposed CAA from the start, they never wanted us to have legal belonging in this country. Biswajit Das is just a flip-flopper representing their desperation. Why would I switch away from the government that finally gave my family what we should have had all along?
primary_drivers: CAA rules implemented and certificates being issued—this validates my 2019 vote
Shantanu's direct role in delivering the CAA promise as Modi's ally
TMC's historical opposition to CAA and opposition to legal belonging for Matua Hindus
